picks.title: Amsterdam Roots Festival
picks.blurb: World music, dance, circus, performances, workshops, food bazaar celebrating global cultures. Free-to-enter diverse stages.
picks.source: I amsterdam, AmsterdamRoots.nl · I amsterdam · I amsterdam, Wittenborg · I amsterdam & Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam / AmsterdamTips.com

picks.title: IJ-Hallen Flea Market
picks.blurb: 750 outdoor stands, vintage, design, crafts. Massive weekend flea market in Noord. Great daytime energy and food stalls.
picks.why: Weekly anchor event, huge selection, free entry
picks.source: AmsterdamTips · AmsterdamTips, Wittenborg · AmsterdamTips.com · AmsterdamTips.com / ijhallen.nl

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb: Handmade jewellery, art, crafts, live music from Amsterdam's small-scale artisans in historic covered market hall.

picks.title: fakemink: A Terrible Beauty – The Tour
picks.blurb: Hip-hop tour stop at Amsterdam's iconic former-church venue. First time here.
picks.source: Songkick · Songkick, Podium Info, Pop Agenda

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe's biggest flea market: 750 outdoor stalls of vintage clothes, antiques, furniture, vinyl and curiosities.
picks.why: Massive, outdoors this season, treasure-hunting weekend.

picks.title: La Pegatina + Primaat
picks.blurb: Punk-cumbia fusion band with local support at Amsterdam's legendary concert venue.

picks.title: Recycle Rally Cup Final
picks.blurb: Kids build & race eco-friendly vehicles from recycled materials. Workshops, swimming, free activities for all ages.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Your Little Black Book / recyclerally.nl

picks.title: Rooftop Tapes
picks.blurb: Rooftop house party with Chelseajoann, Efe Jackson B2B FRADY, and more. DJs until 3am.

picks.title: Wallen Festival
picks.venue: Nieuwmarkt, Red Light District & old city centre
picks.blurb: 90+ activities spanning art, music, workshops, tours, tastings across historic canals and alleyways. Community-curated fourth edition.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · amsterdam.nl / Gemeente Amsterdam

picks.title: Danh Vo: πνεῦμα (Ἔλισσα)
picks.blurb: Vietnamese-Austrian artist's major exhibition exploring spirituality, memory, and cultural inheritance—closing in five weeks.

picks.title: Animalia Amsterdam: Dierportretten
picks.blurb: Final weekend for striking animal portraits exhibition in 17th-century canal house museum.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: World Pride celebration featuring queer filmmakers globally. Eye's tribute to freedom, diversity and LGBTQ+ cinema opens this weekend.
picks.why: Opens Friday; World Pride timing; celebrates queer creativit

picks.title: CIMA Rooftop Bar
picks.blurb: New 2026 rooftop with Mexican cuisine, margaritas, city views. Evening tacos and DJ sets. Lively weekend vibe.
picks.why: Fresh arrival, margarita hotspot, sunset views

picks.title: Beyond the Manosphere: Masculinities Today
picks.blurb: Urgent exhibition interrogating contemporary masculinities through art, video, and installations—wraps early August.

picks.title: Dutch Bio Science Week
picks.blurb: Diverse program exploring groundbreaking health & bioscience developments. Hands-on activities for all ages.
picks.why: Unique science week spanning weekend, family-friendly

picks.title: Bar Dancing Multipla Last Weekend
picks.blurb: Final weekend for legendary garage club with eclectic DJ mix—techno to house—before closure.
picks.why: Last hurrah for beloved club closing permanently

picks.title: In C (Ballet performance)
picks.blurb: Dutch National Ballet and Junior Company perform Philip Glass's minimal masterpiece on both days.

picks.title: Keti Koti: Naar Ndakaaru Zwemmen
picks.blurb: Celebrate and commemorate Keti Koti with stories, conversations, food, and music honouring freedom and heritage.

picks.title: Kho Liang Ie: Mid-Century Modernist
picks.blurb: Dutch designer's furniture, textiles, and interiors showcase post-war modernism at its most elegant and functional.

picks.title: Rolando Bruno
picks.blurb: Rolando Bruno performs at Tolhuistuin's cultural space Sunday evening.
picks.why: Weekend live set at Amsterdam venue

picks.title: Lil Kleine Live 2026
picks.blurb: Exclusive stadium concert by Dutch rap/hip-hop artist in iconic Olympic Stadium.

picks.title: Houthavens Eats
picks.blurb: Outdoor food market with street food, dining and waterfront views at creative Houthavens hub.

picks.title: Escape Amsterdam Club Night
picks.blurb: House and electronic DJs including Raymundo, Dina van Diest, 3NRGY, MC Yanto.

picks.title: Painting Workshop
picks.blurb: Create inspired by Van Gogh's work in the museum's own painting workshop for all skill levels.

picks.title: Encore x Afrolosjes
picks.blurb: Late-night Afrobeat and house party at Amsterdam's iconic concert hall.

picks.title: REM Amsterdam Rooftop Terrace
picks.blurb: Iconic water-level rooftop bar with 360° views of IJ waterfront. Platform on stilts, casual-stylish, packed weekends.
picks.why: Best rooftop-on-water combo, always buzzing weekend

picks.title: WOPL! Summer Festival
picks.blurb: Family-friendly afternoon festival celebrating summer with music, food, and fun activities for all ages.

picks.title: African Food Festival 2026
picks.blurb: African cuisine evening celebrating pan-continental flavors at intimate canal-side venue.
picks.why: Unique themed food event this weekend

picks.title: Escape Amsterdam Saturday Night
picks.blurb: Late-night club session with Raymundo, Dina van Diest, 3NRGY, Danique on percussion.
picks.why: Club running til 5am with live percussion

picks.title: Madam By Night Invites: Jasper Fioole, Vain, Be Lion
picks.blurb: High-floor club night with curated DJ lineup. Views plus late-night house beats.

picks.title: Maker Market at De Hallen
picks.blurb: Creative weekend market: handmade jewelry, art, live music. Covered indoor passage, weekend craft/community hub.
picks.why: Local maker showcase, free, indoor option

picks.title: Food for Thought Festival
picks.blurb: Keti Koti tribute with live music, DJs, spoken word, performances and Caribbean soul food on terraces.

picks.title: Introduction to Ceramics & Pottery
picks.blurb: Hand-building and wheel-throwing ceramics workshop. Shape your own piece—mug, bowl, or plate—with guidance in a supportive studio.

picks.title: ARTIS Summer Programme – New Aquarium
picks.blurb: Water takes centre stage this summer at ARTIS with splashing activities and the brand-new ARTIS Aquarium showcase.

picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: 79th edition finale: theatre, dance, opera, film, music across Amsterdam. Final weekend concentrates multiple productions.
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam, AmsterdamTips · Muziekgebouw & Holland Festival official · I amsterdam & AmsterdamTips

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Powerful photojournalism exhibition closes this weekend. Iconic images documenting global events and human stories.
picks.why: Last chance to see award-winning photography
picks.source: I amsterdam · Amsterdam Spotted & I amsterdam

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book, AmsterdamTips

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: GRIMM – Family Dance Performance
picks.blurb: Hip-hop meets classical ballet in a wild reimagining of fairy tales. Darker, funnier, more spectacular than traditional retellings.
picks.why: Critically praised family spectacle, sells out

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Diageo World Class Cocktail Festival Rotterdam
picks.blurb: 11 premier cocktail bars across Rotterdam host this prestigious mixology festival celebrating craft spirits and skilled bartending.
picks.why: International cocktail festival, expert drinks scene

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: Eye's curated classics programme, screened in 35mm whenever possible. Deep-dive into cinema's foundations.
picks.why: Celluloid experience; canonical works; Eye's expertise

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
